metadata.subject: Mathematics
metadata.description: Foundations of Mathematics 12 is the capstone course for students in the Foundations pathway, developing advanced mathematical thinking through probability and combinatorics, function families (polynomial, exponential, logarithmic, sinusoidal), statistical regression, geometric explorations, and financial planning. This course satisfies the Grade 12 mathematics graduation requirement and prepares students for post-secondary programs in social sciences, business, health, and arts.
metadata.general_description: The capstone Foundations of Mathematics course covering probability, combinatorics, function families, statistical regression, geometric explorations (conics, fractals, constructions), and financial planning — preparing students for post-secondary programs not requiring calculus.

big_ideas.statement: Probabilistic thinking informs decision making in situations involving chance and uncertainty.
big_ideas.elaboration: Combinatorics and probability — from counting methods to conditional probability and expected value — provide mathematical tools for reasoning under uncertainty in contexts from genetics to game theory.

big_ideas.statement: Modelling data requires an understanding of a variety of functions.
big_ideas.elaboration: Different types of relationships in the world are best modelled by different function families — polynomial, exponential, logarithmic, and sinusoidal. Selecting and fitting appropriate models is a key mathematical and scientific skill.
Advanced financial mathematics — including mortgage analysis, investment comparison, and portfolio planning — equips students to make informed, long-term financial decisions.

big_ideas.statement: Through explorations of spatial relationships, we can develop a geometrical appreciation of the world around us.
big_ideas.elaboration: Exploring geometric constructions, conic sections, and fractals reveals mathematical structure and beauty in architecture, nature, and art — deepening spatial reasoning and appreciation for mathematics.

competencies.raw: Develop thinking strategies to solve puzzles and play games; explore, analyze, and apply mathematical ideas using reason, technology, and other tools; estimate reasonably; model with mathematics in situational contexts; think creatively with curiosity.
i_can_statements: I can develop thinking strategies to solve complex counting, probability, and function problems.
I can use graphing technology to explore and model function families and regression analysis.
I can model real-world situations using appropriate function families and probability tools.

competencies.raw: Develop, demonstrate, and apply mathematical understanding through play, story, inquiry, and problem solving; visualize to explore and illustrate mathematical concepts; apply flexible and strategic approaches; solve with persistence and positive disposition; engage in place-based problem-solving.
i_can_statements: I can develop and demonstrate understanding of combinatorics, functions, and probability through inquiry.
I can visualize graphs of polynomial, exponential, logarithmic, and sinusoidal functions.
I can apply flexible strategies to solve probability, regression, and financial problems.

competencies.raw: Explain and justify mathematical ideas and decisions in many ways; represent mathematical ideas in concrete, pictorial, and symbolic forms; use mathematical vocabulary; take risks when offering ideas.
i_can_statements: I can explain and justify probability calculations, function models, and financial decisions.
I can represent functions using graphs, equations, and tables, and choose appropriate forms for context.
I can use accurate mathematical vocabulary in discussions of probability, statistics, and functions.

competencies.raw: Reflect on mathematical thinking; connect concepts with each other, other areas, and personal interests; use mistakes as opportunities to advance learning; incorporate First Peoples worldviews, perspectives, knowledge, and practices.
i_can_statements: I can reflect on my probability and function modelling thinking and refine my strategies.
I can connect mathematics to science, economics, and personal financial planning.
I can incorporate First Peoples geometric and mathematical knowledge — including fractal-like patterns in Indigenous art and seasonal cycles — into my thinking.

areas.name: Combinatorics and Probability
areas.raw: Permutations, combinations, pathways, Pascal's Triangle; probability including binomial and conditional.
topics: Fundamental counting principle: tree diagrams and multiplication rule
Permutations: nPr, arrangements with and without repetition
Combinations: nCr, selecting without regard to order
Pathways problems: counting grid paths
Pascal's Triangle: patterns, binomial coefficients, and row sums
Probability review: experimental vs. theoretical, sample space
Mutually exclusive events: P(A or B) = P(A) + P(B)
Non-mutually exclusive events: inclusion-exclusion principle
Conditional probability: P(A|B) = P(A and B)/P(B)
Independent vs. dependent events
Binomial probability: P(X = k) = nCk × p^k × (1-p)^(n-k)
Expected value: calculating and interpreting
Venn diagrams: representing probability relationships

areas.name: Functions and Regression
areas.raw: Polynomial, logarithmic, exponential, and sinusoidal functions; regression analysis.
topics: Polynomial functions: end behaviour, degree, roots, and graphing
Exponential functions: growth and decay, base e, graphing and applications
Logarithmic functions: inverse of exponential, laws of logarithms, solving equations
Sinusoidal functions: period, amplitude, phase shift, and vertical shift
Regression analysis: choosing the appropriate model (polynomial, exponential, sinusoidal, logarithmic)
Regression using technology: calculating and interpreting r² (coefficient of determination)
Applications: population growth, sound waves, financial modelling, and natural cycles

areas.name: Geometric Explorations
topics: Geometric constructions: perpendicular bisectors, angle bisectors, tangent lines, inscribed polygons, tessellations
Conic sections: parabola, ellipse, circle, hyperbola — locus definitions and equations
Applications of conics: satellite dishes, bridges, orbits, and architecture
Fractals: iteration, self-similarity, and construction of fractal models (Sierpinski triangle, Koch snowflake)
Fractals in nature: coastlines, ferns, snowflakes, and First Peoples geometric art

areas.name: Financial Planning
areas.raw: Personal financial portfolio: mortgages, risk, interest rates, credit cards, banking, financial markets.
topics: Mortgages: principal, interest, amortization periods, and payment calculations
Investment portfolios: diversification, risk tolerance, and long-term growth
Financial markets: overview of stocks, bonds, mutual funds, and ETFs
Credit cards: interest calculations, minimum payments, and total cost of debt
Banking options: comparing accounts, fees, and financial institutions
Personal financial planning: creating a long-term financial plan
